Off Calendar Jan-03-2022 Continued To Feb-28-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DOMINIQUE J. ALEXANDER, AN INDIVIDUAL, DOING MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT

Plaintiff's motion to strike answer is continued to February 28, 2022 to enable defendant to obtain a certificate of revivor. Plaintiff demonstrates that defendant is a suspended LLC that cannot defend this action and defendant does not dispute that contention. (Miller Decl., Exs. 1, 2; Steinsapir Decl. par. 2; Spillane Decl., 5.) Defendant is in the process of making the back payments to obtain a certificate of revivor. (Spillane Decl., pars. 5-7.)

Given these circumstances, the Court finds that a continuance is warranted. The main purpose of the statutory suspension is to collect a tax and parties raising the issue are only "incidental beneficiaries" of that law. (See Color-Vue, Inc. v. Abrams (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 1599, 1605 [trial court abused its discretion by dismissing corporate claims rather than granting a continuance to allow corporation to pay its taxes and obtain certificate of revivor]; Peacock Hill Assn. v. Peacock Lagoon Constr. Co. (1972) 8 Cal.3d 369, 371 ["the purpose of section 23301 of the Revenue and Taxation Code is to put pressure on the delinquent corporation to pay its taxes," and "[t]here is little purpose in imposing additional penalties after the taxes have been paid."].)
